root_text.sn35.165:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe…
root_text.sn35.165:1.2: ekamantaṁ nisinno so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.165:1.3: “kathaṁ nu kho, bhante, jānato kathaṁ passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyatī”ti?
root_text.sn35.165:2.1: “Cakkhuṁ kho, bhikkhu, aniccato jānato passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.165:2.2: Rūpe aniccato jānato passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.165:2.3: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ aniccato jānato passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.165:2.4: Cakkhusamphassaṁ aniccato jānato passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.165:2.5: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccato jānato passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.165:2.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, jānato evaṁ passato micchādiṭṭhi pahīyatī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.165:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.165:1.3: “Sir, how does one know and see so that wrong view is given up?”
translation_text.sn35.165:2.1: “Mendicant, knowing and seeing the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact as impermanent, wrong view is given up. …
translation_text.sn35.165:2.5: And also knowing and seeing the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact as impermanent, wrong view is given up.
translation_text.sn35.165:2.6: This is how to know and see so that wrong view is given up.”
